acceptance.assertion: Only allowlisted realpaths and required shared libraries are visible inside the sandbox.
Authentication context is mounted read-only and narrowly, with secrets absent from receipts and logs.
Claude packet review runs successfully through the formal workspace and sandbox while forbidden paths remain inaccessible.

goal: Allow explicitly approved external agent binaries and bounded authentication context inside the Agent Workspace sandbox without broadening unrelated filesystem access.

does_not_establish: approval of arbitrary external binaries or authentication roots
network isolation from the explicitly required provider endpoint
absence of secrets inside the external provider itself
metadata.reason: PR #193 and PR #199 plus the deployed formal Fable workspace resolved external-agent sandbox execution while preserving bounded isolation.

validation.github_ci: required validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) jobs passed on all cited implementation PRs

validated_acceptance.explicit-binary: PR #193 introduces the explicit Claude profile and bounded binary/library mounts; the post-deploy formal workspace ran through /opt/grabowski-external/claude while a foreign Bureau path remained inaccessible.
validated_acceptance.bounded-auth: The profile mounts the owner-private Claude authentication root read-only; receipts contain hashes and bounded tails rather than credential contents, and symlinked credentials remain rejected.
validated_acceptance.workspace-proof: After PR #199 restored resolver binding, the deployed formal workspace completed writer, tests and independent Fable review on first attempt with PASS and no findings.

metadata.verification_note: PR #193 implemented the explicit external-agent profile, PR #199 fixed the host-managed resolv.conf bind, merge ff0290 was deployed with all integrity flags valid, and the formal post-deploy Fable workspace closed successfully.

title: Expose approved external agents inside Workspace sandbox
